other_observers: Greg Cook; Greg Cook of Arlington, TX found and photographed the bird. I reached him by phone and arrived 20 minutes later. He re-found the bird upon my arrival. We lost track of the bird shortly after my observation, and to my knowledge was not seen again by a
specific_location: Castlewood Canyon SP, along Cherry Creek ~300 yds north of dam ruins.
description: A dendroica warbler, yellow below, greenish-yellow above with blackish, broken streaking down the sides and flanks and a dark spot on the side of the neck near the top of the blackish streaks. Olive green wings with thin, yellow wingbars. A dark, eyeline with yellow crescents above and below the eye, and a dark olive colored mustacial crescent, becoming wider and paler towards the rear, eventually blending with the greenish-yellow nape and head color. No red streaking was noted on the back during observation, however a photo shows a possible hint of pale red streaks.Behaviors: Flitting in the willows, staying mostly out of sight, responsing briefly to pishing.Call: none heardPlumage: adult
